ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on anchor elements
ai.description: The selector `a` sets outline: none without providing a visible focus indicator replacement such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline in the base rule or paired focus rules (lines 62 and 300 provide only colour change and underline, which do not satisfy the requirement for a visible focus ring). Keyboard users cannot see when links receive focus.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on input elements
ai.description: The selector `input:focus` sets outline: none and only changes border-color to #ddd, which is identical to the default border colour on line 190, failing to provide a visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when text inputs receive focus.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on select elements
ai.description: The selector `select:focus` sets outline: none with only a border-color of #ddd (identical to the default), providing no visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when select elements receive focus.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on textarea elements
ai.description: The selector `textarea:focus` sets outline: none with only a border-color of #ddd (identical to the default), providing no visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when textareas receive focus.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on button elements
ai.description: The selector `button` sets outline: none without ensuring all buttons have a visible focus replacement. While some buttons have specific focus styles (lines 79-80, 225-226), others such as generic buttons or those added dynamically lack a visible focus indicator.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #cta-btn
ai.description: The selector `#cta-btn:focus` sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. Keyboard users cannot see when this specific button receives focus.

ai.issueType: Focus indicator removed without replacement on section links
ai.description: The selector `section a:focus` sets outline: none and provides only text-decoration: underline, which is not a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline as required for a visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when these links receive focus.

The colour #999 on the default background #fff produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1, which is below the minimum 4.5:1 required for normal text. Users with low vision cannot read this text.

ai.issueType: Insufficient colour contrast on header navigation links
ai.description: The colour #0066ff on background #003366 produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which is below the minimum 4.5:1 required for normal text. Users cannot read the navigation links.

ai.issueType: Insufficient colour contrast on header navigation buttons
ai.description: The colour #0066ff on background #003366 produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which is below the minimum 4.5:1 required for normal text. Users cannot read the button text.

ai.issueType: Insufficient colour contrast on primary CTA button
ai.description: The background colour #ff6b6b with white text (#fff) produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1, which is below the minimum 4.5:1 required for normal text. Users cannot read the button text.

ai.issueType: Insufficient colour contrast on input placeholders
ai.description: The placeholder colour #999 on the input background produces a contrast ratio below 4.5:1. Since the associated labels are hidden (line 206), these placeholders serve as the only visible labels and must meet contrast requirements.

ai.issueType: Link underline removed without alternative on header navigation
ai.description: The rule `text-decoration: none` on `header nav a` removes underlines from links without providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as font-weight change, border-bottom, or background highlight in the default state. Users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.

ai.issueType: Link underline removed without alternative on section links
ai.description: The rule `text-decoration: none` on `section a` removes underlines without a non-colour visual differentiator in the default state (underline only appears on hover/focus). Users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.
A transition is defined on #cta-btn outside of a prefers-reduced-motion media query. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from this animation.

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Background and foreground colors do not have a sufficient contrast ratio.
lighthouse.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn how to provide sufficient color contrast](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/color-contrast).

lighthouse.issueType: Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order
lighthouse.description: Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. [Learn more about heading order](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/heading-order).

lighthouse.issueType: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
lighthouse.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more about the `alt` attribute](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/image-alt).
